docs.title: The Enterprise School Finance OS
docs.text:
Remitpro automates school fee collection, academic management, HR, payroll, and compliance for Nigerian educational institutions — all under one multi-tenant SaaS platform.

₦200 Platform fee / tx 25+ DB tables 13 API endpoints 15 Service classes

docs.title: Quick Navigation
docs.text:
🛠️ Developer Onboarding New to the codebase? Start here — environment setup, architecture overview, and your first PR in a day.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js 20+ — nodejs.org
  • Git — git-scm.com
  • Neon account — neon.tech (free tier works)
  • Monnify Sandbox — sandbox.monnify.com

docs.title: 1. Clone & Install
docs.text:
git clone git@github.com:remitpro/schoolpay.git
cd schoolpay
npm install --legacy-peer-deps
cp .env.example .env
# → Edit .env with your credentials

docs.title: 2. Database Setup
docs.text:
npm run db:push      # Sync schema to Neon
npm run db:rls       # Apply Row Level Security
npm run db:triggers  # Setup DB triggers
npm run db:seed      # Load demo data (Greenwood International)

docs.title: 3. Start Dev Servers
docs.text:
# Terminal 1: Express backend
npm run server:dev   # Starts on http://localhost:5001

# Terminal 2: Vite frontend
npm run dev          # Starts on http://localhost:8080

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text:
Open http://localhost:8080 → click "Sign in as Demo Admin" → you should see the Greenwood dashboard.

# Quick health check
curl http://localhost:5001/health
# → {"status":"ok","timestamp":"..."}

Full Setup Guide

See the Developer Onboarding Guide for a detailed walkthrough of all setup steps, environment variables, and architecture concepts.

docs.title: API Reference & Integration Guide 🔌
docs.text: Remitpro provides a suite of internal and external API endpoints via a Node.js/Express server to power the multi-tenant school operations, payment services, and messaging workflows.

docs.title: 🏗️ Base Configuration
docs.text:
• Local Development URL: http://localhost:5001/api
  • Production URL: https://[YOUR_EXPRESS_HOST]/api
  • Authentication: Custom JWT Auth Token (sent via Authorization: Bearer <JWT>). User login, registration, and sessions are completely database-driven and managed by the Express microservices.

docs.title: 1. Register School & Owner (POST /api/auth/signup)
docs.text:
Registers a new school tenant and creates the default owner account in the profiles table. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "email": "owner@school.com",
  "password": "SecurePassword123!",
  "fullName": "Adebayo Adesina",
  "schoolName": "Lagos Academic Center",
  "subdomain": "lagos-academic",
  "phone": "+2348031112222",
  "address": "12 Broad St, Lagos"
}
- Response: 200 OK 
{
  "success": true,
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJI...",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid-string-here",
    "email": "owner@school.com",
    "fullName": "Adebayo Adesina",
    "role": "owner",
    "schoolId": "uuid-school-id"
  }
}

docs.title: 2. User Login (POST /api/auth/login)
docs.text:
Authenticates user credentials and issues a custom JWT access token. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "email": "user@school.com",
  "password": "SecurePassword123!"
}
- Response: 200 OK (returns success, token, and user profile data).

docs.title: 3. Get Active Session (GET /api/auth/session)
docs.text: Retrieves the logged-in user profile from the database based on the provided JWT bearer token. - Method: GET - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <JWT> - Response: 200 OK (returns the user profile data).

docs.title: 4. Accept User Invitation (POST /api/auth/accept-invite)
docs.text:
Completes registration for a new user by verifying their invitation JWT token and saving their password credentials. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "token": "invitation-jwt-token-here",
  "password": "UserChosenPassword123!",
  "fullName": "Chioma Nze"
}
- Response: 200 OK (returns session token and user profile data).

docs.title: 5. Demo Login (POST /api/auth/demo-login)
docs.text:
Provisions (or re-activates) the built-in Greenwood International School demo workspace and returns a valid session token. This endpoint is idempotent — it can be called repeatedly without errors regardless of DB state (upserts school + profile, always refreshes password hash). - Method: POST - Payload: (none required) - Response: 200 OK 

{
  "success": true,
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJI...",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid-string-here",
    "email": "info@greenwood.edu.ng",
    "fullName": "Demo Admin",
    "role": "owner",
    "schoolId": "uuid-school-id",
    "schoolSubdomain": "greenwood"
  }
}

Demo Credentials

Email: info@greenwood.edu.ng | Password: password123 The demo workspace is pre-seeded with students, fees, grades, and expenses.

docs.title: 6. Onboard School (POST /api/onboard-school)
docs.text:
Initializes a new school workspace, provisions their tenant record in Neon, and sets up a Monnify bank sub-account for split remittances. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <Admin_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "name": "Greenwood International School",
  "email": "admin@greenwood.edu.ng",
  "phone": "+2348031234567",
  "address": "12 High School Rd, Lagos",
  "bankName": "Wema Bank",
  "accountNumber": "0123456789",
  "accountName": "Greenwood Edu Services"
}
- Response: 200 OK 
{
  "success": true,
  "schoolId": "uuid-string-here",
  "subAccountCode": "MFY_SUB_..."
}

docs.title: 7. Generate Virtual Account (POST /api/generate-virtual-account)
docs.text:
Provisions a dedicated bank virtual account (Wema/Rolez) for a student via the Monnify API. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <User_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "studentId": "uuid-student-id",
  "schoolId": "uuid-school-id"
}
- Response: 200 OK returning details of the reserved bank account.

docs.title: 8. Invite Staff User (POST /api/invite-user)
docs.text:
Initiates the onboarding/RBAC invite flow for bursars and other staff members by issuing a secure JWT-based invitation token. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <Admin_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "email": "bursar@greenwood.edu.ng",
  "role": "bursar",
  "schoolId": "uuid-school-id"
}
- Response: 200 OK (invitation link generated and sent to the staff member).

docs.title: 9. Verify Monnify Payment (POST /api/verify-payment)
docs.text:
Performs a direct server-to-server transaction verification against the Monnify API and processes the student ledger sync. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <User_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "transactionReference": "SP-TX-1004321"
}
- Response: 200 OK with verified payment confirmation details.

docs.title: 10. Monnify Webhook Handler (POST /api/monnify-webhook)
docs.text: Processes transaction notifications securely received from Monnify's transaction servers. - Method: POST - Headers: monnify-signature (HMAC-SHA512 checksum validating the payload against the merchant secret) - Payload: Standard Monnify webhook JSON notification payload containing transactionReference, amountPaid, paymentStatus, etc. - Response: 200 OK (Processes splits, sets student ledger status to Paid, and emits system alerts).

docs.title: 11. Promote Students (POST /api/promote-students)
docs.text:
Performs batch promotion of students to the next class levels and rolls over the term cycle. - Method: POST - Headers: Authorization: Bearer <Admin_JWT> - Payload: 

{
  "schoolId": "uuid-school-id",
  "promotions": [
    { "fromClass": "JSS1", "toClass": "JSS2" },
    { "fromClass": "JSS2", "toClass": "JSS3" }
  ]
}
- Response: 200 OK (Returns summary of promoted student counts).

docs.title: 12. Send Proxy Email (POST /api/send-email)
docs.text:
Enables transactional mailing (via Resend) without exposing server API keys to frontend assets. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "to": "recipient@domain.com",
  "subject": "Greenwood Onboarding Invite",
  "html": "<p>Invite body...</p>"
}
- Response: 200 OK

docs.title: 13. Send Proxy SMS (POST /api/send-sms)
docs.text:
Enables transactional mobile SMS sending (via Termii) to parents and staff. - Method: POST - Payload: 

{
  "to": "+2348000000000",
  "message": "Dear Parent, fee payment of ₦50,000 was successfully processed."
}
- Response: 200 OK

docs.title: 📡 Webhook/Realtime Specifications
docs.text: If you wish to subscribe to payment events asynchronously, configure listener endpoints against Supabase Realtime listening on the transactions table.

Emitted automatically when a virtual account payment settles.

  • Enterprise Tier: Scalable clusters with custom throughput settings.

docs.title: 1. Executive Summary
docs.text: Remitpro is a multi-tenant SaaS platform designed for Nigerian educational institutions to manage finances, academics, HR, and operations. Built on a modular service architecture, the system leverages React (Vite) on the frontend, a Node.js/Express backend (microservices), and Neon (PostgreSQL) managed via Drizzle ORM to deliver a secure, scalable, and offline-capable solution.

docs.title: 1.1 Key Architectural Characteristics
docs.text: Characteristic Implementation Multi-Tenancy Row-Level Security (RLS) with school_id isolation Security Model Zero-trust with client-side RLS + server-side validation Data Integrity Immutable financial logs, audit trails, ACID compliance Offline Support PWA with Service Workers + IndexedDB sync queue Scalability Modular microservices, Neon connection pooling, indexed queries Compliance Nigerian financial regulations (WHT, Pension, TRCN)

docs.title: 2. Architectural Principles
docs.text: The system is built on the following core principles:

docs.title: 2.1 Design Principles
docs.text:
1. Security by Default: All data access controlled by RLS; sensitive operations in Express Microservices Backend
  2. Offline-First: Critical workflows (attendance) function without internet connectivity
  3. Financial Integrity: Immutable transaction logs; double-entry accounting principles
  4. Type Safety: End-to-end TypeScript with specific types for all service methods and component props. Avoidance of the any keyword in favor of strict typing and unknown error handling.
  5. Multi-Tenant Isolation: Strict data segregation at database level
  6. Auditability: Comprehensive logging of all state-changing operations
  7. Progressive Enhancement: Core features work on basic browsers; enhanced on modern ones

docs.title: 2.2 Architectural Patterns
docs.text:
• Client-First Architecture: Direct database access from frontend (secured by RLS)
  • CQRS-Lite: Separate read/write paths for complex financial operations
  • Event-Driven: Webhooks and database triggers for async workflows
  • Service Layer Pattern: Business logic encapsulated in TypeScript services
  • Repository Pattern: Drizzle ORM abstracts database operations

docs.title: 5.1 Express Microservices Backend
docs.text: The backend business logic and secure API integrations run on a modular Node.js/Express server (located in server/) written in TypeScript.

docs.title: Client Request Interception
docs.text: To maintain compatibility and prevent client refactoring, all supabase.functions.invoke(...) calls from the React frontend are dynamically intercepted in client.ts and rerouted to the Express server gateway (http://localhost:5000/api locally, or VITE_MICROSERVICES_URL in production). Active session JWT tokens are forwarded in the Authorization header so the server can securely verify caller identities.

docs.title: Endpoints / Route Handlers
docs.text:
The Express server registers 10 separate routes corresponding to the migrated functions. These routes query the Neon PostgreSQL database directly using Drizzle ORM instead of going through PostgREST/Supabase client:

Route Path HTTP Method Authentication Purpose /api/onboard-school POST Service Role / Admin JWT Create school + provision Monnify subaccount in Neon DB /api/invite-user POST Admin JWT Elevate staff role, create DB profile, send Resend invite link /api/generate-virtual-account POST User JWT (Fallback) Create student reserved Monnify account & store details /api/monnify-webhook POST Webhook Signature Process payment notification & mark ledger paid /api/webhook-payment POST Webhook Signature Alternate route for webhook validation /api/verify-payment POST User JWT Server-to-server Monnify transaction check and ledger sync /api/promote-students POST Admin JWT Run academic term rollover and promote classes in Neon /api/send-email POST Service Role Proxy route to send email using Resend API /api/send-sms POST Service Role Proxy route to send SMS notifications using Termii API /api/monnify-create-account POST Service Role Base virtual account setup

docs.title: Example: Payment Webhook Security
docs.text:
// server/src/routes/webhookPayment.ts
import { Router } from 'express';
import crypto from 'crypto';

const router = Router();

router.post('/', async (req, res) => {
  const payload = req.body;
  const signature = req.headers['monnify-signature'];

  // Verify webhook authenticity using HMAC-SHA512
  const computedSignature = crypto
    .createHmac('sha512', process.env.MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY || '')
    .update(JSON.stringify(payload))
    .digest('hex');

  if (signature !== computedSignature) {
    return res.status(401).send('Invalid signature');
  }

  // Process payment directly in Neon via Drizzle ORM...
  await db.insert(transactions).values({
    studentId: payload.productCode,
    amount: payload.amountPaid,
    transactionReference: payload.transactionReference,
    // ... audit details
  });

  return res.status(200).send('OK');
});

docs.title: 5.2 Service Layer Pattern
docs.text:
Business logic is encapsulated in 15 TypeScript services:

// Example: FeeService.ts
export class FeeService {
  static async generateLedgerEntries(
    studentId: string,
    classLevel: string,
    term: string
  ): Promise<void> {
    // 1. Fetch master fee schedule for class
    const schedule = await supabase
      .from('master_fee_schedule')
      .select('*')
      .eq('class_level', classLevel);

    // 2. Create ledger entries for student
    const entries = schedule.data.map(fee => ({
      student_id: studentId,
      fee_schedule_id: fee.id,
      fee_name: fee.name,
      amount: fee.amount,
      term: term,
      is_paid: false
    }));

    await supabase.from('student_ledgers').insert(entries);

    // 3. Audit log
    await AuditService.log('fee_generation', 'student', studentId);
  }
}

Service Responsibilities: - AcademicService: Grade calculations, report cards, class positions - FeeService: Ledger generation, payment allocation - PayrollService: Salary calculations, deductions (pension, WHT) - NotificationService: Multi-channel messaging (Email, SMS, In-App) - WalletService: Parent wallet management, balance tracking - AuditService: Compliance logging

docs.title: 6.2 Financial Data Model
docs.text:
The financial system uses a modified double-entry approach:

Master Fee Schedule (Template)
        ↓
Student Ledgers (Debt Tracker)
        ↓
Transactions (Immutable Payment Log)

Key Principles: 1. Master Fee Schedule: Single source of truth for fees per class 2. Student Ledgers: Individual debt records (mutable: amount_settled) 3. Transactions: Immutable payment history (never deleted/updated) 4. Balance Calculation: outstanding_balance = SUM(ledger.amount - ledger.amount_settled)

docs.title: 6.3 Indexing Strategy
docs.text:
-- High-traffic query optimization
CREATE INDEX ledger_school_idx ON student_ledgers(school_id);
CREATE INDEX ledger_student_idx ON student_ledgers(student_id);
CREATE INDEX ledger_unpaid_idx ON student_ledgers(school_id, is_paid);
CREATE INDEX transactions_created_idx ON transactions(created_at);
CREATE INDEX students_created_idx ON students(created_at);

-- Unique constraints for data integrity
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX school_id_student_id_idx ON students(school_id, student_id);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX student_position_idx ON class_positions(student_id, session, term);

docs.title: 6.4 Data Retention & Archival
docs.text: Data Type Retention Archive Strategy Transactions Permanent Never delete (compliance) Audit Logs 7 years Move to cold storage after 2 years Academic Records Permanent Archived per session Attendance 5 years Aggregate to monthly summaries Notifications 90 days Delete after read + 90 days

docs.title: 7.1 Multi-Tenant Isolation (RLS)
docs.text:
Row-Level Security is the cornerstone of data isolation:

-- Example RLS Policy for students table
CREATE POLICY "school_isolation_policy" ON students
  USING (school_id = (
    SELECT school_id FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()
  ));

-- Super-admin bypass
CREATE POLICY "super_admin_access" ON students
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM profiles 
      WHERE id = auth.uid() 
      AND role = 'super_admin'
    )
  );

RLS Coverage: - ✅ All tables with school_id have isolation policies - ✅ profiles table links auth.uid() to school_id - ✅ Super-admin role can bypass for platform monitoring - ✅ Policies enforced at PostgreSQL level (not application)

docs.title: 7.2 Authentication & Authorization
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[User Login] --> B{Supabase Auth}
    B -->|Success| C[JWT Token]
    C --> D[Frontend Stores Token]
    D --> E[API Requests Include Token]
    E --> F{RLS Check}
    F -->|Authorized| G[Return Data]
    F -->|Denied| H[403 Forbidden]

Auth Flow: 1. User authenticates via Supabase Auth (email/password) 2. JWT token issued (contains user_id) 3. Token included in all API requests 4. PostgreSQL RLS uses auth.uid() from JWT 5. Data filtered by school_id automatically

Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):

Role Permissions Implementation super_admin Platform-wide access RLS bypass policy admin Full school access Standard RLS teacher Read students, write grades Application-level checks accountant Finance module only Application-level checks parent View own children only Custom RLS on student_id

docs.title: 7.3 Payment Security
docs.text:
Monnify Integration Security: 1. Webhook Signature Validation: HMAC-SHA512 verification 2. Idempotency: transaction_reference unique constraint prevents duplicates 3. Split Settlement: Platform fee deducted at gateway level 4. PCI Compliance: No card data stored (Monnify handles)

Virtual Account Security: - Accounts generated server-side only (/api/generate-virtual-account route) - Account numbers permanently linked to student_id - Cannot be reassigned or deleted

docs.title: 7.4 Data Protection
docs.text: Layer Protection Mechanism Transport TLS 1.3 (HTTPS) Storage PostgreSQL encryption at rest Secrets Environment variables (never committed) API Keys Hidden in Express microservices backend (not exposed to client) Sensitive Fields NIN, TIN encrypted in database Audit Trail All mutations logged to audit_logs

docs.title: 8.3 Integration Resilience
docs.text:
Retry Strategy: 

async function retryWithBackoff(fn, maxRetries = 3) {
  for (let i = 0; i < maxRetries; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (error) {
      if (i === maxRetries - 1) throw error;
      await new Promise(resolve => 
        setTimeout(resolve, Math.pow(2, i) * 1000)
      );
    }
  }
}

Circuit Breaker: Implemented for external APIs to prevent cascade failures

docs.title: 10.1 Performance Optimizations
docs.text: Layer Optimization Impact Frontend Code splitting (React.lazy) -40% initial bundle size Frontend Image optimization (WebP) -60% image payload Frontend React Query caching -80% redundant API calls Database Strategic indexing 10x faster queries Database Connection pooling Handles 1000+ concurrent users API Express API routes & connection pool Sub-100ms response times CDN Vercel Edge Network Global <50ms latency

docs.title: 10.2 Scalability Considerations
docs.text:
Current Capacity: - Schools: 1,000+ tenants - Students: 100,000+ records - Concurrent Users: 5,000+ - Transactions/day: 50,000+

Scaling Strategy: 1. Horizontal Scaling: Render/Railway auto-scales Express app instances 2. Database Scaling: Read replicas for reporting queries 3. Caching: Redis layer for hot data (future) 4. CDN: Static assets distributed globally

  .from('students')

docs.text: Phase 1 (Current): Monolithic SPA + Node/Express Microservices (Neon DB) Phase 2 (6 months): Dedicated worker microservices for heavy workloads (reporting, analytics) Phase 3 (12 months): Event-driven architecture with message queues (e.g. RabbitMQ / Redis) Phase 4 (18 months): Multi-region deployment for global expansion

docs.title: Appendix A: Technology Decisions
docs.text: Decision Rationale Neon & Supabase over Firebase PostgreSQL (relational) better for financial data; RLS built-in/managed on Neon. Vite over Create React App 10x faster builds; modern tooling Drizzle over Prisma Lighter weight; better TypeScript inference Shadcn/UI over Material-UI Unstyled primitives; full design control React Query over Redux Server state management; less boilerplate

docs.title: Appendix B: Glossary
docs.text:
• RLS: Row-Level Security (PostgreSQL feature)
  • PWA: Progressive Web App
  • CQRS: Command Query Responsibility Segregation
  • WHT: Withholding Tax (Nigerian tax regulation)
  • TRCN: Teachers Registration Council of Nigeria
  • NIN: National Identity Number (Nigeria)

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to Remitpro are documented here.

Format follows Keep a Changelog, versioning follows Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Demo Login — Replaced fragile signup fallback with idempotent POST /api/auth/demo-login endpoint. Repeated demo logins no longer fail due to unique constraint violations on the greenwood school record. The endpoint atomically upserts the school and profile on every call.
  • db:push command — Upgraded drizzle-kit from v0.18.1 to v0.31.10 to restore the push subcommand that was removed in intermediate versions.

docs.title: Improved
docs.text: • MkDocs — Full enterprise documentation upgrade: navigation instant loading, toc.follow, social links, repo_url, cookie consent, custom CSS/JS, feedback widget, and new developer pages (Onboarding, Codebase Map, DB Schema, Troubleshooting, Contributing, Changelog).

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Staff Appraisal System — Formal termly performance review workflow with objective tracking and score history.
  • Curriculum Repository — Teachers upload lesson plans and schemes of work; admins approve and publish.
  • Bulk Grade Import — CSV/Excel template upload for CA and Exam scores directly in the Grades module.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Incorrect audit log labels in AttendanceService.ts causing misleading compliance records.
  • Student daily roll-call unique index constraint to prevent duplicate entries per day.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Parent Wallet Engine — Internal prepaid wallet for every student. Parents fund via Monnify VA; admins can debit wallet for fees, store items, or transport.
  • WHT (Withholding Tax) Support — Supplier bills now include WHT rate, computed deduction, and net_amount as a PostgreSQL generated column.
  • Batch Payslip Download — ZIP generation for entire payroll runs so admins can download all payslips at once.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • current_session default updated from 2023/2024 to 2024/2025 in schema.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Computer Based Testing (CBT) — Quiz builder for teachers with timed multi-choice questions. Student exam mode with auto-scoring.
  • Broadcast Center — Admin dashboard for SMS/Email blasts to filtered audiences (class, all parents, all staff).
  • Remittance Intelligence Hub — Super-admin dashboard showing ₦200/transaction earnings across all schools, projected revenue, and collection rate benchmarks.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• PDF ID Card Engine — Generates student and staff ID cards in PVC vertical format with school branding.
  • Global Fast-Search — ⌘K / Ctrl+K shortcut searches students, staff, and fees from any page.
  • Custom School Branding — Admins can upload school logos that appear on PDFs, portals, and ID cards.

docs.title: Improved
docs.text: • Dark mode transitions — smoother glassmorphism animations and high-contrast mode compliance.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Smart Attendance (Biometric) — Geofencing + facial liveness check for staff clock-in via mobile browser. GPS coordinates and attendance photo stored and displayed in the admin dashboard.
  • Student Daily Roll-Call — Teachers mark daily student attendance from any device.
  • Offline-First Attendance — Service Worker queues roll-call data locally when offline; auto-syncs when reconnected.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Race condition in virtual account generation where duplicate Monnify API calls caused duplicate account numbers.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Express.js Backend Migration — All 10 Supabase Edge Functions (Deno) ported to Node.js/Express routes. Backend now deployed on Fly.io with direct Drizzle ORM connections to Neon PostgreSQL.
  • Custom JWT Authentication — Replaced Supabase GoTrue with bcryptjs + jsonwebtoken for full multi-tenant token control. client.ts intercepts all supabase.auth.* calls.
  • DB Proxy Route — Universal POST /api/db-proxy proxies all table CRUD with automatic school_id scoping, replacing PostgREST.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: • JWT now embeds school_id in every token payload, preventing cross-tenant data access at the application layer.

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
• Authentication system replaced: Supabase GoTrue → Custom JWT. Existing user sessions are invalidated. All users must log in again.
  • supabase.functions.invoke() now routes to Express backend. Edge Functions are deprecated.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Multi-tenant Subdomain System — Each school gets a unique subdomain (school.remitpro.io). Tenant context is derived from URL hostname.
  • Payroll System — Full payroll run management with line items, status tracking, and PDF payslips.
  • Inventory & Store — School store with SKU management, stock logging, and sales recording.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Academic Result Publishing — Admin "Publish Results" toggle gates parent portal access. Parents can download PDF report cards.
  • Class Positions — Automatic ranking calculation (1st, 2nd, 3rd...) after grade entry.
  • Behavioral Skills Ratings — Teacher assessments for soft skills (Punctuality, Neatness, Handwriting) on student report cards.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Supplier Management — Track suppliers, issue bills, record partial payments.
  • Expense Tracking — Log operational expenses with categories, receipts, and approval status.
  • P&L Dashboard — Real-time Profit & Loss: Income (collected) − Expenses − Payroll.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Staff Invite System — Role-based staff invitations via JWT-secured email links. Staff set their own password on first login.
  • RBAC Enforcement — Role-based access control enforced at the UI level and API middleware.
  • Audit Logs — All sensitive mutations (fee changes, manual payments, role promotions) logged to audit_logs table.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Monnify Virtual Accounts — Per-student dedicated Wema Bank virtual accounts provisioned via Monnify API.
  • Webhook Payment Processing — Monnify webhook validates HMAC-SHA512 signature, updates ledger, settles ₦200 platform fee.
  • Payment Receipt Emails — Automated receipts via Resend API on payment confirmation.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Master Fee Schedule — Define fees by class level and category. Mandatory vs optional flagging.
  • Student Ledgers — Automatic debt generation from fee schedule when term starts.
  • Bulk CSV Import — Upload student lists via CSV with validation and duplicate detection.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Multi-tenant school management SaaS foundation
  • School onboarding wizard (2-step: profile + bank account)
  • Student directory with CRUD operations
  • Supabase Auth + PostgreSQL (Neon) with Row Level Security
  • React + Vite + shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS frontend
  • PWA (Progressive Web App) with service worker
  • "Indigo-Night" design system with dark mode support
  • Super-Admin platform dashboard
  • Parent Portal (no-login access via Student ID + phone)

For security vulnerability reports, email security@remitpro.io privately.

docs.title: Contributing Guidelines
docs.text: Thank you for contributing to Remitpro! This guide covers everything you need to know about contributing code, documentation, or bug reports.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text:
We expect all contributors to:

  • Be respectful and constructive in code reviews
  • Write clear commit messages and PR descriptions
  • Not commit secrets, credentials, or personally identifiable data
  • Test changes before submitting a PR

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
See the Developer Onboarding Guide for full setup instructions.

TL;DR: 

git clone git@github.com:remitpro/schoolpay.git && cd schoolpay
npm install --legacy-peer-deps
cp .env.example .env   # Fill in values
npm run db:push && npm run db:rls && npm run db:triggers && npm run db:seed
npm run server:dev     # Terminal 1: backend on :5001
npm run dev            # Terminal 2: frontend on :8080

docs.title: Branching Strategy
docs.text:
main          ← production (protected, requires PR)
dev           ← integration (requires PR from feature branches)
feat/NAME     ← new features (branch from dev)
fix/NAME      ← bug fixes (branch from dev)
hotfix/NAME   ← urgent prod fixes (branch from main, PR into both main + dev)
docs/NAME     ← documentation changes

docs.title: Creating a Branch
docs.text:
# Always branch from dev (unless it's a hotfix)
git checkout dev
git pull origin dev
git checkout -b feat/my-feature-name

docs.title: Commit Convention
docs.text:
We follow Conventional Commits:

<type>(<scope>): <short description>

[optional body]
[optional footer: e.g. BREAKING CHANGE, Refs #123]

docs.title: Types
docs.text: Type When to Use feat New feature for users fix Bug fix docs Documentation only style Formatting, no logic change refactor Code refactoring, no feature/fix test Add or fix tests chore Build process, dependency updates perf Performance improvement

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
feat(finance): add partial payment recording to ledger
fix(auth): handle null schoolId for super-admin users
docs(api): document POST /auth/demo-login endpoint
chore(deps): upgrade drizzle-kit from 0.18 to 0.31
refactor(services): extract fee calculation to FeeService
test(payroll): add unit tests for WHT calculation

docs.title: Before Opening a PR
docs.text:
• Code compiles: npm run build
  • Lint passes: npm run lint
  • Unit tests pass: npm run test
  • New features have at least one unit test
  • Sensitive operations log to AuditService
  • New DB tables have RLS policies in setup_rls.ts
  • No secrets or .env values committed

docs.title: PR Template
docs.text:
When opening a PR, include:

## What does this PR do?
Brief description of the change.

## Why?
Context or link to issue: Fixes #123

## How to test
1. Start dev servers
2. Navigate to X
3. Do Y
4. Verify Z

## Screenshots (if UI change)
Before | After

## Checklist
- [ ] Tests added/updated
- [ ] Docs updated if needed
- [ ] No secrets committed
- [ ] Linting passes

docs.title: Review Requirements
docs.text:
• At least 1 approval required to merge into dev
  • At least 2 approvals required to merge into main
  • All CI checks must pass (lint, build, unit tests)
  • No unresolved review comments

docs.title: TypeScript
docs.text:
// ✅ Use specific types, not 'any'
const student: typeof students.$inferSelect = result;

// ✅ Use Zod for input validation
const schema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), amount: z.number().positive() });
const parsed = schema.parse(req.body);

// ✅ Handle errors uniformly
try {
  const data = await someAsyncOp();
  return res.json({ success: true, data });
} catch (err: unknown) {
  const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unexpected error';
  return res.status(500).json({ error: message });
}

// ⛔ Don't use 'any'
const data: any = response;  // Bad

// ⛔ Don't silently swallow errors
try { ... } catch (_) {}  // Bad

docs.title: React Components
docs.text:
// ✅ Use React Query for server state
const { data, isLoading, error } = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['students', schoolId],
  queryFn: () => supabase.from('students').select('*'),
  staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,
});

// ✅ Use services, not direct Supabase calls in components
const result = await FeeService.generateLedger(studentId, class, term);

// ✅ Always show loading + error states
if (isLoading) return <LoadingSpinner />;
if (error) return <ErrorMessage error={error} />;

// ⛔ Don't fetch in useEffect (use React Query)
useEffect(() => {
  supabase.from('students').select('*').then(...);  // Bad
}, []);

docs.title: API Routes
docs.text:
// ✅ Always use authMiddleware on protected routes
router.post('/my-route', authMiddleware, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res) => {
  const { schoolId, role } = req.user!;

  // ✅ Always scope queries by schoolId
  const data = await db.select().from(myTable).where(eq(myTable.schoolId, schoolId));

  // ✅ Check role when needed
  if (role !== 'admin' && role !== 'owner') {
    return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Insufficient permissions' });
  }
});

// ⛔ Don't skip schoolId scoping
const data = await db.select().from(students);  // Returns ALL students — data breach risk!

docs.title: Database Changes
docs.text:
// ✅ Use Drizzle ORM — never raw SQL strings in routes
const student = await db.select().from(students)
  .where(and(eq(students.schoolId, schoolId), eq(students.id, studentId)))
  .limit(1);

// ✅ Log mutations to AuditService
await AuditService.log({
  action: 'student_updated',
  entityType: 'student',
  entityId: student.id,
  userId: req.user!.id,
  schoolId,
  details: JSON.stringify({ changes }),
});

docs.title: Unit Tests (Vitest)
docs.text:
Place tests in src/test/ or alongside the file as *.test.ts.

// src/services/FeeService.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import { FeeService } from '@/services/FeeService';

describe('FeeService', () => {
  it('calculates outstanding balance correctly', () => {
    const result = FeeService.calculateOutstanding(50000, 20000);
    expect(result).toBe(30000);
  });
});

docs.title: E2E Tests (Playwright)
docs.text:
Place tests in tests/.

// tests/auth.spec.ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test('demo login works', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('http://localhost:8080/login');
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /sign in as demo/i }).click();
  await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/dashboard/);
});

Run all tests: 

npm run test        # Vitest unit tests
npm run test:e2e    # Playwright E2E tests

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
When your PR adds or changes behaviour, update the relevant docs page in docs/:

If you change... Update... An API endpoint docs/developer/api-reference.md The DB schema docs/developer/database-schema.md A deployment step docs/developer/deployment.md A user-facing feature docs/user-guides/school-admin.md A known issue/fix docs/developer/troubleshooting.md

Preview docs locally: 

npm run docs:serve

docs.title: Versioning
docs.text:
The project uses Semantic Versioning:

  • MAJOR (3.0.0): Breaking API or schema change
  • MINOR (2.6.0): New feature, backward compatible
  • PATCH (2.5.3): Bug fix

Update package.json version on each release and add an entry to docs/developer/changelog.md.

Contributing Guide v1.0 | Remitpro Engineering

docs.title: 🚀 Production Deployment Guide: Remitpro
docs.text: This guide outlines the professional workflow for deploying the Remitpro multi-tenant SaaS to a production environment using a Node.js/Express microservices server and a Neon PostgreSQL database.

docs.title: 📋 Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Neon Account (for PostgreSQL serverless database hosting).
  • A Supabase Account (for Auth/Identity and Object Storage).
  • A Monnify Live Merchant Account (for payment processing and split billing).
  • Node.js 20+ installed locally.
  • A Render, Railway, or Heroku account for backend API server hosting.
  • A Vercel or Netlify account for frontend hosting.

docs.title: 1. Neon Database Setup
docs.text:
1. Create a new project in your Neon Dashboard.
  2. Note your DATABASE_URL connection string (ensure it uses pooled connections -pooler for production scaling).

docs.title: 2. Supabase Project (Auth & Storage)
docs.text:
1. Create a new project in the Supabase Dashboard.
  2. Note your Project URL, API Keys (both anon and service_role).
  3. Enable standard Supabase Auth configurations (site URL, SMTP settings).
  4. Create a bucket named attendance-photos in the Supabase Storage console and set it to public or apply proper RLS.

docs.title: 3. Monnify Live Setup
docs.text:
1. Log in to your Monnify Live Dashboard.
  2. Obtain your Merchant Code, API Key, and Secret Key.
  3. Create a Main Subaccount for platform fees and note the subAccountCode.

docs.title: 🗄️ Step 2: Database & Schema Migration
docs.text: Remitpro uses Drizzle ORM for schema management. Do not run SQL tables scripts manually unless executing structural triggers.

docs.title: 1. Run Schema Migration
docs.text:
Set your production DATABASE_URL in your local environment, then push the schema: 

# Push the schema definitions to Neon PostgreSQL
npm run db:push

docs.title: 2. Apply Security & Rules
docs.text:
Apply the Row-Level Security (RLS) policies and triggers: 

# 1. Apply multi-tenant security policies (RLS)
npm run db:rls

# 2. Setup system triggers (e.g. auth sync profiles and default sessions)
npm run db:triggers

docs.title: 3. Initial Seeding (Optional)
docs.text:
If provisioning default templates, seed data: 

npm run db:seed

docs.title: ⚡ Step 3: Express Backend Deployment
docs.text: The backend server is a Node.js Express application located in server/. You can deploy this to Render, Railway, Fly.io, or any Node-compatible container service.

docs.title: 1. Configure Server Environment Variables
docs.text: On your backend host dashboard (e.g., Render Web Service or Fly.io secrets), set the following environment variables: - DATABASE_URL: Your production Neon PostgreSQL connection string. - SUPABASE_URL: Your production Supabase URL. - SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: Your production Supabase service role API key. - MONNIFY_API_KEY: Your production Monnify API Key. - MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY: Your production Monnify Secret Key. - MONNIFY_BASE_URL: https://api.monnify.com (Live URL) - PLATFORM_SUBACCOUNT_CODE: Your subaccount code for collecting the ₦200 platform fee. - RESEND_API_KEY: Your Resend API key (for transaction receipts/invites). - TERMII_API_KEY: Your Termii API key (for SMS broadcasts). - PORT: 5001 (or leave default if the hosting provider assigns it automatically).

docs.title: 2. Backend Build Settings
docs.text:
• Build Command: npm install (run from the root)
  • Start Command: npx tsx server/src/index.ts (or build to JS and run node server/dist/index.js for production scale).

docs.title: 3. Deploying specifically to Fly.io
docs.text:
We configure Fly.io using the Dockerfile, .dockerignore, and fly.toml files at the root of the project: 1. Log in to the Fly CLI: 

fly auth login
2. Create the application: 
fly apps create remitpro-backend --org remitpro-ltd
3. Set your production secrets securely: 
fly secrets set DATABASE_URL="..." VITE_SUPABASE_URL="..." MONNIFY_API_KEY="..." MONNIFY_SECRET_KEY="..." MONNIFY_BASE_URL="https://api.monnify.com"
4. Deploy the application: 
fly deploy --remote-only

docs.title: 💻 Step 4: Frontend Deployment (Vercel)
docs.text:
1. Connect your GitHub repository to Vercel.
  2. Configure the Build & Development Settings:
  3. Build Command: npm run build
  4. Output Directory: dist
  5. Framework Preset: Vite
  6. Add the following Environment Variables in Vercel:
  7. VITE_MICROSERVICES_URL: The URL of your deployed Express backend server on Fly.io: https://remitpro-backend.fly.dev/api.
  8. VITE_SUPABASE_URL: Your Production Supabase URL (for storage).
  9. VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: Your Production Supabase Anon/Publishable Key.
  10. Vercel automatically honors the vercel.json file in the root directory to handle SPA routing redirects correctly.

docs.title: 👮 Step 5: Platform Owner Initialization
docs.text:
Once the frontend and backend are live: 1. Navigate to your live site and Sign Up with your owner email. 2. Go to your local terminal and run the admin elevation script against the production DB: 

# Update OWNER_EMAIL in .env to match your signup email
npx tsx src/db/promote_admin.ts
3. Your account is now elevated to a platform-wide Super-Admin.

docs.title: 🛡️ Step 6: Post-Deployment Checklist
docs.text:
1. Webhook Configuration: In the Monnify Dashboard, set your live Webhook URL to point to the Express backend endpoint: https://remitpro-backend.fly.dev/api/monnify-webhook
  2. CORS Security: Ensure your Supabase project's Site URL and Redirect URLs match your production frontend domain.
  3. SSL: Ensure your frontend and Express backend are served exclusively over HTTPS.

docs.title: 📈 Monitoring & Maintenance
docs.text:
• System Logs: Inspect the logs of your Express backend server on Fly.io (e.g. using fly logs --app remitpro-backend in the CLI) for payment hook delivery and user invitation statuses.
  • Neon DB Health: Use the Neon console to monitor active connections, auto-scaling CPU usage, and slow query execution plans.
  • Database Backups: Configure automatic point-in-time recovery (PITR) directly in Neon console.

For critical infrastructure support, contact the Remitpro DevOps team.

docs.title: Developer Onboarding Guide
docs.text: 🛠️ Engineering

docs.title: Welcome to Remitpro Engineering
docs.text:
This guide gives a new engineer everything they need to clone the repo, understand the codebase, and ship their first change within a single working day. Read it end-to-end before touching any code.

Time to complete: ~3–4 hours including environment setup.

docs.title: 1. Project Context
docs.text: Remitpro is a production multi-tenant SaaS built for Nigerian educational institutions. It automates school fee collection via bank virtual accounts (Monnify), manages academics, HR, payroll, and student records.

docs.title: Business Model
docs.text:
Every fee payment → Monnify splits instantly:
  ├── School subaccount  → Remainder (e.g. ₦49,800)
  └── Platform account  → Flat ₦200 fee (our revenue)

docs.title: Key Stakeholders
docs.text: Role Who Description Super-Admin Platform owner (Remitpro Ltd.) Sees all schools, monitors revenue School Admin / Owner School principal or registrar Full control over one school workspace Bursar Finance staff Manages fees, payments, expenses Teacher Academic staff Enters grades, marks attendance Parent Student's guardian Portal-only; pays fees, views results

docs.title: 2. Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure the following tools are installed:

RequiredRecommendedAccounts Needed Tool Minimum Version Install Node.js 20 LTS nodejs.org npm 10+ Bundled with Node Git 2.40+ git-scm.com Python 3.10+ python.org (for MkDocs) Tool Purpose VSCode Primary IDE with workspace config TablePlus / DBeaver Inspect Neon PostgreSQL Bruno / Insomnia API testing Drizzle Studio (npm run db:studio) Visual DB browser

You will need read access to these services (ask the platform owner):

  • GitHub — remitpro/schoolpay repository
  • Neon — dev database (separate from prod)
  • Monnify Sandbox — payment testing
  • Supabase — storage & legacy auth reference
  • Fly.io — production backend hosting

docs.title: 3.1 Clone & Install
docs.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone git@github.com:remitpro/schoolpay.git
cd schoolpay

# Install all dependencies (frontend + backend are in the same repo)
npm install --legacy-peer-deps

Legacy Peer Deps Flag

The --legacy-peer-deps flag is required due to a peer dependency conflict between @sentry/react and the latest React ecosystem. This is tracked in the technical debt register.

docs.title: 3.2 Environment Variables
docs.text:
Copy the template and fill in your values:

cp .env.example .env

Then edit .env. Below is a complete reference:

.env — Complete Reference
# ── Frontend (Vite prefix = exposed to browser) ──────────────
# Supabase project URL — used for file storage only
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project.supabase.co

# Supabase anon/publishable key (safe to expose)
VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=eyJhbGci...

# Points the React app at your local Express backend
VITE_MICROSERVICES_URL=http://localhost:5001/api

# ── Backend (Server-side only, never in browser) ─────────────
# Neon PostgreSQL connection string (use the POOLER URL)
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@ep-xxx-pooler.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require

# Monnify credentials (use SANDBOX values for dev)
MONNIFY_API_KEY=MK_TEST_...
MONNIFY_API_SECRET=...
MONNIFY_API_URL=https://sandbox.monnify.com

# Your platform's Monnify sub-account (₦200 fee destination)
PLATFORM_SUBACCOUNT_CODE=MFY_SUB_...

# Notification services
RESEND_API_KEY=re_...
TERMII_API_KEY=TL...

# Super-admin elevation target email
OWNER_EMAIL=admin@remitpro.io

# JWT secret — MUST be a strong random string in production
JWT_SECRET=change-me-in-production-32chars-min

# Express server port
PORT=5001

Never Commit .env

The .env file is listed in .gitignore. Never commit real credentials. Use fly secrets set for production and GitHub Secrets for CI.

docs.title: 3.3 Database Setup (Dev)
docs.text:
# 1. Push the Drizzle schema to your Neon dev database
npm run db:push

# 2. Apply Row Level Security policies
npm run db:rls

# 3. Create database triggers (auto-profile sync, audit hooks)
npm run db:triggers

# 4. Seed demo data (Greenwood International School)
npm run db:seed

Use the Dev Branch DB

The project has two Neon databases:

  • Dev → Used for local development (in .env)
  • Prod → The live system (in .env with the # prod comment)

Never point your local DATABASE_URL at production.

docs.title: 5.1 The Mock Supabase Client (src/integrations/supabase/client.ts)
docs.text:
Most Important File in the Codebase

This is the #1 thing that confuses new developers. Read this section carefully.

The project started with Supabase but migrated to a custom Express/Neon backend. Rather than refactoring every component, client.ts intercepts all Supabase API calls and proxies them to the Express server.

Component calls:          → Actually goes to:
supabase.auth.signIn()    → POST http://localhost:5001/api/auth/login
supabase.auth.signUp()    → POST http://localhost:5001/api/auth/signup
supabase.from('students') → POST http://localhost:5001/api/db-proxy
supabase.functions.invoke → POST http://localhost:5001/api/{function-name}

What this means for you: - Supabase Auth is NOT used for authentication - All auth is JWT-based via the Express server (server/src/auth-helper.ts) - All DB queries go through the /api/db-proxy route (server/src/routes/dbProxy.ts) - The real Supabase client is only used for file storage (attendance photos, uploads)

docs.title: 5.2 Authentication Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Browser
    participant client.ts as client.ts (Mock)
    participant Express as Express :5001
    participant Neon as Neon PostgreSQL

    Browser->>client.ts: supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({email, password})
    client.ts->>Express: POST /api/auth/login
    Express->>Neon: SELECT * FROM profiles WHERE email = ?
    Neon-->>Express: profile row (with passwordHash)
    Express->>Express: bcrypt.compare(password, hash)
    Express-->>client.ts: { token: "eyJ...", user: {...} }
    client.ts->>Browser: localStorage.setItem('remitpro-session-token', token)
    Browser->>Browser: window.location → subdomain redirect

The JWT token is stored in localStorage under key remitpro-session-token and sent as Authorization: Bearer <token> on every subsequent API request.

docs.title: 5.3 Multi-Tenancy & Subdomain Routing
docs.text:
Each school gets a unique subdomain (e.g., greenwood.localhost:8080). The useSchoolSettings hook reads the subdomain from window.location.hostname to determine which school's data to display.

// src/utils/subdomain.ts
export function slugifySubdomain(text: string): string { ... }
export function buildSubdomainUrl(subdomain: string, path: string, token?: string): string {
  // In dev: greenwood.localhost:8080/?token=...
  // In prod: greenwood.remitpro.io/?token=...
}

docs.title: 5.4 The DB Proxy (/api/db-proxy)
docs.text:
All supabase.from('tableName').select/insert/update/delete() calls from the frontend go through dbProxy.ts. The proxy:

  1. Reads the JWT token from the Authorization header
  2. Verifies it to get schoolId and role
  3. Automatically scopes all queries to the user's school_id
  4. Executes the query against Neon via Drizzle ORM

This is the application-level multi-tenancy enforcement. RLS at the PostgreSQL level provides a second layer.

docs.title: 5.5 The ₦200 Revenue Model
docs.text:
When a student's virtual account receives a transfer, Monnify sends a webhook to /api/monnify-webhook. The server:

  1. Validates the HMAC-SHA512 signature
  2. Deducts ₦200 as platform_fee in the transactions table
  3. Updates the student's ledger (student_ledgers.amount_settled)
  4. Recalculates students.outstanding_balance
  5. Sends a receipt notification

docs.title: 6.1 Adding a New Page
docs.text:

    <Route path="/my-new-page" element={<MyNewPage />} />

docs.title: 6.2 Adding a New API Endpoint
docs.text:
1. Create server/src/routes/myNewRoute.ts: 

    import { Router } from 'express';
import { authMiddleware } from '../auth-helper';
import { db } from '../db';

const router = Router();

router.post('/my-endpoint', authMiddleware, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { schoolId } = req.user!;
    // ... query Neon via Drizzle
    return res.json({ success: true });
  } catch (err: any) {
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

export default router;

  2. Register it in server/src/index.ts: 

    import myNewRouter from './routes/myNewRoute';
app.use('/api', myNewRouter);

docs.title: 6.3 Adding a New Database Table
docs.text:
1. Add the table definition to src/db/schema.ts (Drizzle ORM)
  2. Push the schema change: 
    npm run db:push
  3. If the table needs RLS, add a policy in src/db/setup_rls.ts and run: 
    npm run db:rls
  4. Export the table in the import lists used in dbProxy.ts and seed.ts

Migration vs Push

db:push applies schema changes directly (dev-friendly). For production, generate migrations first: 

npm run db:generate  # Creates SQL in /drizzle/
# Review the generated SQL, then:
npm run db:push

import { FeeService } from '@/services/FeeService';
const ledger = await FeeService.generateLedgerEntries(studentId, class, term);

// ⛔ Avoid — direct DB call in component
const { data } = await supabase.from('student_ledgers').insert({...});

docs.title: 8. Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
docs.text: These are key decisions made before you joined. Understanding them prevents re-litigating solved problems.

docs.title: ADR-001: Custom Auth over Supabase Auth
docs.text:
Decision: Replace Supabase GoTrue authentication with a custom JWT system (bcrypt + jsonwebtoken).

Reason: Supabase Auth does not natively support the multi-tenant subdomain model where a school_id must be embedded in every JWT claim. The custom system gives full control over the token payload.

Impact: supabase.auth.* calls in the UI are intercepted by client.ts and proxied to Express.

docs.title: ADR-002: Express Backend over Supabase Edge Functions
docs.text:
Decision: Migrate all Supabase Edge Functions (Deno) to a Node.js/Express server deployed on Fly.io.

Reason: Deno Edge Functions had cold-start latency, limited npm support, and poor debugging tooling. Node/Express with Drizzle gives full TypeScript support, local debugging, and direct DB access.

Impact: supabase.functions.invoke('name', ...) calls are intercepted by client.ts and proxied to http://localhost:5001/api/{name}.

docs.title: ADR-003: Neon PostgreSQL over Supabase Postgres
docs.text:
Decision: Use Neon as the primary database, with Supabase used only for storage and the legacy client interface.

Reason: Neon provides branching, autoscaling, and a better pricing model for multi-tenant workloads. Supabase PostgREST was replaced by the DB proxy to maintain query control.

docs.title: ADR-004: Drizzle ORM over Prisma
docs.text:
Decision: Use Drizzle ORM for all database access.

Reason: Drizzle generates zero overhead at runtime, has superior TypeScript inference, and produces readable SQL. Prisma's abstraction layer and migration model were incompatible with direct Neon + RLS workflows.

docs.title: ADR-005: Application-Level Tenancy via DB Proxy
docs.text:
Decision: All CRUD queries are scoped to school_id at the application layer (dbProxy.ts), backed by PostgreSQL RLS as a second enforcement layer.

Reason: Pure RLS requires passing auth.uid() through the connection, which doesn't work cleanly with direct Drizzle connections. The two-layer approach (app + DB) provides defense-in-depth.

docs.title: 9. Branching & Code Review
docs.text:
main        ← production-ready code (protected)
dev         ← integration branch (merge features here first)
feat/*      ← feature branches (branch from dev)
fix/*       ← bug fix branches
hotfix/*    ← urgent prod fixes (branch from main)

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Branch from dev: git checkout -b feat/my-feature dev
  2. Write code & tests
  3. Run npm run lint && npm run test locally
  4. Open PR → dev (never directly to main)
  5. Request review from at least 1 team member
  6. Squash merge after approval

feat(finance): add partial payment support to ledger
fix(auth): handle expired JWT in session middleware
docs(api): add demo-login endpoint reference

Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore

Setting Local Dev Staging Production Frontend URL http://localhost:8080 https://staging.remitpro.io https://app.remitpro.io Backend URL http://localhost:5001 https://remitpro-backend-staging.fly.dev https://remitpro-backend.fly.dev Database Neon Dev branch Neon Staging Neon Production Monnify Sandbox Sandbox Live Auth JWT (local secret) JWT (Fly secret) JWT (Fly secret) Frontend port 8080 (Vite) 443 (Vercel) 443 (Vercel)

Frontend Dev Port

docs.title: Key Metrics to Monitor
docs.text: Metric Where to Check Backend errors fly logs --app remitpro-backend DB query performance Neon Console → Query analytics Frontend errors Sentry (configured via @sentry/react) Uptime UptimeRobot / Pingdom Payment webhooks Monnify Dashboard → Webhook logs

docs.title: Common Performance Traps
docs.text:
• N+1 queries in the DB proxy: The dbProxy.ts handles one table at a time. Complex joins should be handled in dedicated Express routes, not through the proxy.
  • Large CSV imports: Student bulk imports are processed synchronously. For >500 students, this can time out. Future: move to a background job.
  • Attendance photo uploads: Photos go to Supabase Storage. Keep bucket policies correct or uploads will silently fail.

docs.title: 12. Security Checklist for New Features
docs.text:
Before merging any PR, verify:

  • New API routes use authMiddleware (never skip auth on non-demo routes)
  • DB queries are scoped by school_id from req.user.schoolId
  • No secrets are logged to console.log
  • Input is validated (use Zod schemas)
  • New tables have RLS policies added to setup_rls.ts
  • Sensitive mutations call AuditService.log()
  • CORS is not widened beyond * without documented reason

docs.title: Remitpro: Deep Technical Manual 🛠️
docs.text: This document provides architects and developers with a deep-dive into the internal workings of the Remitpro platform.

docs.title: Logical Isolation (Row Level Security)
docs.text:
Remitpro uses a shared-database, shared-schema multi-tenant model. Isolation is strictly enforced at the database level using PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) on Neon.

  • Global Context: The auth.uid() of the requester is used to fetch the school_id from the profiles table.
  • Enforcement: Most tables have an ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY command applied.
  • Policies: 
    CREATE POLICY school_isolation_policy ON students 
USING (school_id = (SELECT school_id FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()));
  • Super-Admin Bypass: Super-admins have a distinct role in the profiles table that allows them to bypass RLS for platform-wide monitoring.

docs.title: 2. Database Schema (Drizzle ORM)
docs.text:
The schema is designed for high financial integrity and auditability. Key entities include:

  • Schools: Primary tenant entity. Stores Monnify sub-account codes and academic cycle settings.
  • Profiles: Extended user data. Links individual Auth users to a school.
  • Master Fee Schedule: The "Rulebook" for a school. Defines what each class (e.g., JSS1) should pay.
  • Student Ledgers: The "Debt Tracker". When a term starts, entries are generated here based on the Master Schedule.
  • Transactions: Immutable records of every Naira that enters the system.

docs.title: 💰 3. The "₦200 Flat-Fee" Revenue Model
docs.text: Remitpro implements a unique revenue model where the platform owner earns ₦200 on every transaction.

docs.title: Implementation Logic
docs.text:
The split is handled during the Monnify Transaction Initialization: 1. Request: The system requests a transaction for ₦10,000. 2. Split Rule: - Platform Subaccount: ₦200 (Fixed) - School Subaccount: ₦9,800 (Remainder) 3. Settlement: Monnify handles the split instantly upon payment.

Note: This is managed within the generate-virtual-account and monnify-webhook microservice routes.

docs.title: ⚡ 4. Microservices (Node/Express Server) & Integrations
docs.text: The backend backend services run on a Node/Express server (located in server/) written in TypeScript, connecting directly to the Neon PostgreSQL database using Drizzle ORM. Frontend invocations of supabase.functions.invoke(...) are intercepted in client.ts and redirected to http://localhost:5001/api/<function-name>.

docs.title: onboard-school
docs.text:
• Trigger: Called manually from the Onboarding UI.
  • Route: POST /api/onboard-school
  • Logic: Creates the schools record in Neon and asynchronously calls Monnify to provision a subAccountCode. Links the current user as the admin of that school.

docs.title: generate-virtual-account
docs.text:
• Trigger: Triggered when a staff member creates a virtual account for a student.
  • Route: POST /api/generate-virtual-account
  • Logic: Provisions a reserved account (Wema/Rolez) via Monnify. The account is permanently mapped to the student's studentId and saved to Neon.

docs.title: monnify-webhook
docs.text:
• Trigger: Incoming HTTP POST from Monnify.
  • Route: POST /api/monnify-webhook
  • Logic:
  • Validates the Monnify Hash Signature.
  • Identifies the student via the productCode or transactionReference.
  • Updates the student_ledgers directly in Neon (marking debts as paid).
  • Records the transactions entry for auditing.

docs.title: verify-payment
docs.text:
• Trigger: Client-side widget callback or manual admin check.
  • Route: POST /api/verify-payment
  • Logic: Queries Monnify API directly (Server-to-Server) to confirm status of a transaction reference. Updates the Neon DB directly only if valid. Sends receipt via send-email.

docs.title: invite-user
docs.text:
• Trigger: Admin sends invitation.
  • Route: POST /api/invite-user
  • Logic: Handles Supabase Auth invitation via the administrative SDK, assigns multi-tenant schoolId and role in Neon, and sends branded onboarding email via Resend.

docs.title: send-email
docs.text:
• Trigger: Internal Service Call (supabase.functions.invoke).
  • Route: POST /api/send-email
  • Logic: Proxies requests to Resend API. Keeps API keys hidden from client. Log-only mode if keys missing.

docs.title: promote-students
docs.text:
• Trigger: Admin "End Session" action.
  • Route: POST /api/promote-students
  • Logic:
  • Batches students by class.
  • Updates class string (e.g., JSS1 -> JSS2) in Neon.
  • Archives current academic records for historical reporting.
  • Resets currentTerm to TERM_1.

docs.title: 5.1 Resilience & Offline Sync
docs.text: The platform implements an Offline-First strategy for high-frequency operations: - Service Worker (PWA): Uses vite-plugin-pwa to cache assets and core UI, allowing the app to launch without internet. - Attendance Queueing: Uses OfflineStore (LocalStorage) to capture student roll-calls. - Background Sync: A useEffect hook monitors the online window event. Upon reconnection, it automatically upserts the local queue to Neon and clears the cache.

docs.title: 5.2 Biometric Clock-In Logic
docs.text:
• Geofencing: Utilizes geolib to compare the teacher's current coordinates against the school's configured geofence_lat/lng. Access to the camera is only granted if the distance is within the geofence_radius.
  • Liveness Verification: Implemented a multi-state machine for biometric capture, requiring the user to smile/blink to generate the storage-ready attendance photo.

docs.title: 5.3 Institutional Ecosystem Services
docs.text:
• CurriculumService: Manages the versioned repository of academic documents. Documents are stored in protected Supabase buckets with RLS metadata.
  • AnnouncementService: Centralized engine for class-based and school-wide broadcasts.
  • QuizService: Handles the logic for Computer Based Testing, including randomized question rendering and automated result computation.

docs.title: Indigo-Night Design System
docs.text: The platform utilizes a custom-built design system based on: - Primary Color: Modern Indigo (hsl(243 75% 59%)). - Surface Logic: Glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-xl) with high-transparency card backgrounds. - Dark Mode: Persisted using next-themes and the class attribute on the <html> element.

docs.title: State-of-the-Art Visuals
docs.text:
• Mesh Gradients: Used in the Login/Onboarding background for professional "Fintech" depth.
  • Dynamic Logo Inversion: The .brand-logo utility automatically inverts logo colors for dark backgrounds using CSS filters.

docs.title: 📈 7. Audit & Compliance
docs.text:
• Audit Logs: Sensitive actions (fee changes, manual payment records) are captured in the audit_logs table.
  • Immutability: Once a transaction is recorded, it cannot be deleted or modified through the application UI.

docs.title: 🛡️ 8. Type Safety & Error Handling
docs.text: Starting with v1.2, the platform enforces strict TypeScript conventions to eliminate "runtime surprises."

docs.title: 8.1 Specific Typing over any
docs.text:
• Data Entities: Components use Supabase-generated Tables types from @/integrations/supabase/types.
  • Service Methods: All service methods (e.g., PayrollService, StaffService) are strictly typed to ensure predictable data flow.
  • Mutations: React Query mutations use typeof formData or specific interfaces to validate inputs before submission.

docs.title: 8.2 Robust Error Boundaries
docs.text:
All asynchronous operations use a standardized error-catching pattern: 

try {
  // async operation
} catch (err: unknown) {
  const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "A persistent error occurred";
  toast.error(message);
}
This ensures that the user always receives a human-readable explanation of failures, and the system never silently crashes due to malformed error objects.

Technical Manual v2.0 | © 2026 Remitpro Technologies

docs.title: Manual Testing & QA Guide: Remitpro 🧪
docs.text: This guide provides a comprehensive framework for manual Quality Assurance (QA) to ensure the platform meets enterprise financial standards and multi-tenant security requirements.

docs.title: 📋 1. Testing Prerequisites
docs.text: Before starting, ensure you have: - Access to a Neon development database. - Access to a Supabase project (for Auth/Storage). - A Monnify Sandbox account (for API testing). - Local Express backend running via npm run server:dev (on port 5001). - Local React development server running via npm run dev. - Clean database state (no existing schools/students).

docs.title: Session A: Self-Onboarding & Identity
docs.text:
Objective: Verify a new school can enter the ecosystem and provision their workspace.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result A1 Register a new account with valid institutional details. User is redirected to /onboarding automatically. A2 Complete Step 1 (Profile) and Step 2 (Bank Details). Workspace is created; Monnify sub-account code is generated in DB. A3 Attempt to bypass Onboarding by visiting / directly after signup. AuthGuard intercepts and redirects back to /onboarding. A4 Logout and Login again. Dashboard loads instantly for the provisioned workspace.

docs.title: Session B: Student & Fee Management
docs.text:
Objective: Verify the "Finance Core" can handle institutional rules.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result B1 Create a "Tuition Fees" category for the current term. Category appears in the Fee Structure list. B2 Add a mandatory fee for "JSS1" class (₦50,000). The fee is saved to the master_fee_schedule with correct frequency. B3 Bulk Import 10 students via CSV. All students appear in directory with correct metadata (Class, Enrollment ID). B4 Verify "Initial Debt": Check a student ledger after import. Student should automatically have the ₦50,000 debt mapped to their term.

docs.title: Session C: Virtual Accounts & Payments
docs.text:
Objective: Test the "Fintech Engine" (Sandbox Mode).

Task ID Task Description Expected Result C1 Click "Generate Virtual Account" for a student. System calls Monnify; displays a bank name (Wema) and account number. C2 Mock a Payment: Use the Monnify Sandbox to simulate a transfer. Webhook is triggered; Transaction entry is created in Neon DB. C3 Verify Debt Clearance: Re-check student ledger. Student's isPaid status changes to true instantly. C4 Partial Payment Test: Simulate a payment of ₦20,000 for ₦50,000 debt. Ledger shows ₦30,000 outstanding; status is "Partial".

docs.title: Session D: Multi-Tenant Isolation (SECURITY CRITICAL)
docs.text:
Objective: Ensure School A cannot see School B's data.

Task ID Task Description Expected Result D1 Create School A and Student X. Record created successfully. D2 Open a new incognito window, create School B. School B Dashboard should show 0 students (Student X hidden). D3 Attempt to access Student X's ID directly via URL in School B's session. System returns 404 or "Access Denied" via RLS.

E2 Navigate through the Dashboard in Light Mode. Glassmorphism blur is crisp; shadows feel soft and diffused. E3 Test Sidebar Responsiveness. docs.title: 📈 3. QA Reporting Template
docs.text:
When documenting a bug, use the following format:
